name: chokepoint-edr-bypass
bodyMarkdown:
# EDR Bypass Techniques — Detection Chokepoint

**Priority:** CRITICAL | **Tactic:** Defense Evasion | **Difficulty:** HIGH | **Prevalence:** HIGH
**Techniques:** T1562.001, T1562.006, T1055.001, T1014

## Overview
Adversaries impair EDR/AV before executing payloads. Admin/SYSTEM privileges required; bypass mechanism always produces kernel-observable artifacts.

## Chokepoint Stages

### 1. Privilege Escalation
Admin/SYSTEM privileges required. Observable: Sysmon EID 1 (privilege escalation), WEL 4672 (Special Privilege Logon).

### 2. EDR Telemetry Disruption
Options: kernel driver load (BYOVD), kernel memory modification (callback removal), userland hook patching (ntdll unhooking), ETW/AMSI patching, WFP network blocking, Safe Mode boot.
Observable: Sysmon EID 6 (driver load), EID 7 (ntdll fresh copy), WEL 5441 (WFP filter), EID 1 (bcdedit safeboot).

### 3. Security Process Impairment
Must stop/kill/blind the EDR agent.
Observable: Process termination of security processes, EID 7036 (service state change), sc.exe/net.exe stop commands.

## Variations (14 tracked)
EDRKillShifter, Terminator (Spyboy), AuKill, POORTRY/STONESTOP, Kernel Callback Removal (EDRSandblast), PPL Abuse (PPLKiller), User-mode Unhooking, Direct/Indirect Syscalls (SysWhispers), ETW Patching, AMSI Bypass, EDRSilencer, Module Stomping, SafeMode Boot, BlackSanta.

## Detection Logic
- **Research:** All non-Microsoft kernel driver loads (EID 6)
- **Hunt:** Security process handle requests (EID 10) + service stop/disable (EID 7036/7040)
- **Analyst:** EID 10 + WEL 7036 (service stopped) + WEL 7040 (start type changed)

## Prevention
- Block known EDR-killer binaries by name/hash/publisher
- Enforce HVCI + Microsoft Vulnerable Driver Blocklist
- Deploy Windows Credential Guard / VBS
